pi_items.source: Task description states 'Exactly 4 vessels' and verifier.py checks 'if num_vessels == 4'
Task description specifies 'Position 50.800 N'; verified within Solent region (50.77-50.82°N) via web search on Solent coordinates
Task description specifies '1.150 W (-1.150)'; verified within Solent region (-1.10 to -1.29°W) via web search on Solent coordinates
Task description specifies 'Heading 270'; verified as due West in maritime compass convention; verifier.py checks 'abs(own_hdg - 270) < 10'
Task description specifies 'Speed 12.0 kts'; verified as typical merchant vessel speed (10-15 knots); verifier.py checks 'abs(own_spd - 12) < 2'
ARPA (Automatic Radar Plotting Aid) is a real feature in Bridge Command simulator (https://www.bridgecommand.co.uk/); Bridge Command documentation confirms ARPA support
Full Radar is a valid Bridge Command radar setting found in example bc5.ini config files in the environment
max_radar_range=48 found in example bc5.ini file at  Bridge Command documentation confirms radar range configuration

pi_items.verified_value: Solent is a real maritime location in southern England between Isle of Wight and mainland coast (50.7853°N, -1.2903°W approximately)
pi_items.source: Web search on 'Solent UK maritime location' confirmed via Wikipedia, Britannica, and GPS coordinate sources
Bridge Command INI documentation (https://www.bridgecommand.co.uk/Doc/IniFileFormat.php) specifies times in 24-hour decimal hours; verifier.py checks '(0 <= st <= 4) or (22 <= st <= 24)'

pi_items.verified_value: VisibilityRange 2.0-4.0 nm is reasonable for coastal maritime conditions
pi_items.source: Bridge Command INI format uses VisibilityRange parameter; verifier.py checks '2.0 <= vis <= 4.0'
Bridge Command INI format uses Weather parameter; verifier.py checks 'if wx <= 1.5'

pi_items.metadata_value: Head-on, Crossing from Starboard, Overtaking, Crossing from Port
pi_items.verified_value: COLREGS Rules 13 (overtaking), 14 (head-on), 15 (crossing) are real maritime collision avoidance rules
pi_items.source: Web search on 'COLREGS collision avoidance rules' confirmed via IMO and official navigation regulations; verifier.py analyzes vessel positions for these encounter types

pi_items.metadata_value: Calculate CPA and TCPA for each target
pi_items.verified_value: CPA (Closest Point of Approach) and TCPA (Time to CPA) are standard maritime calculations used in ARPA systems
pi_items.source: Web search on 'maritime radar plotting ARPA CPA TCPA' confirmed via Wikipedia, ScienceDirect, and maritime training resources
0.05 nm = approximately 100 meters; reasonable tolerance for coastal navigation positioning
1.0 knot tolerance is standard for maritime navigation speed verification
5.0 degree heading tolerance is standard for maritime navigation; comparable to gyrocompass accuracy
0.5 nm tolerance for radar range measurements is reasonable for maritime radar precision
5.0 degree bearing tolerance is standard for radar bearing accuracy
0.5 nm CPA tolerance is reasonable for collision avoidance calculations
3.0 minute TCPA tolerance is reasonable for time-to-closest-approach calculations

privileged_info_summary: The task uses Bridge Command, a real open-source maritime simulator, to create a radar plotting training exercise in the Solent, a real strait in southern England. The own ship position (50.800°N, -1.150°W) is verified within the Solent region. The task requires exactly 4 traffic vessels configured for COLREGS Rule 13 (overtaking), Rule 14 (head-on), and Rule 15 (crossing) encounters—all real maritime collision avoidance rules. ARPA (Automatic Radar Plotting Aid) is a real radar feature, and CPA/TCPA calculations are standard maritime ARPA outputs. The ownship heading (270°, due West), speed (12.0 knots), and all specified tolerances are realistic maritime values. The radar setting max_radar_range=48 nm is verified in Bridge Command configuration files. The data is not synthetic—it is based on a real simulator, real geography, and realistic maritime parameters.
